title.zh: 阴阳五行理论详解：中医养生的核心密码
title.en: Yin-Yang & Five Elements: Core Principles of TCM Wellness

description.zh: 深入理解中医阴阳五行理论，掌握五脏六腑与五行的对应关系，开启智慧养生之门
description.en: Deep understanding of Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory, the foundation of TCM wellness

zh.text: 阴阳五行学说是中医理论的核心，它解释了宇宙万物的运行规律和人体内部的生理机制。理解阴阳五行，是学习中医养生的第一步。
阴阳是宇宙万物的两种基本属性，它们相互对立、相互依存、相互转化。阴阳平衡是健康的基础，阴阳失调则会导致疾病。
人体也是一个小宇宙，同样存在阴阳之分：
五行即金、木、水、火、土五种基本物质，它们之间存在相生相克的关系，构成了宇宙万物的循环规律。
相生是指一种物质对另一种物质的滋生、助长作用：

items: 木生火：木性温暖，火隐伏其中
火生土：火燃烧后化为灰烬为土
土生金：土中孕育金属矿石
金生水：金属可化为液体
水生木：水滋润树木生长

zh.text: 相克是指一种物质对另一种物质的制约、克制作用：
中医将人体五脏与五行相对应，形成了独特的藏象学说：

items: 木行体质：多吃绿色食物，保持心情舒畅
火行体质：多吃红色食物，避免情绪激动
土行体质：多吃黄色食物，饮食规律
金行体质：多吃白色食物，保持呼吸道通畅
水行体质：多吃黑色食物，节制性生活

zh.text: 当五行失衡时，会出现各种健康问题，需要进行相应的调理：

items: 肝木过旺：易怒、头痛、失眠 → 疏肝理气
心火过旺：口干、心烦、口舌生疮 → 清心泻火
脾土虚弱：乏力、腹胀、便溏 → 健脾祛湿
肺金不足：咳嗽、气短、易感冒 → 补肺益气
肾水不足：腰酸、耳鸣、脱发 → 滋阴补肾

zh.text: 温馨提示：阴阳五行理论博大精深，需要长期学习和实践才能领会其精髓。养生调理应根据个人体质进行，最好在专业中医师指导下进行。

en.text: Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory is the core of TCM, explaining the operating laws of the universe and the physiological mechanisms within the human body. Understanding Yin-Yang and Five Elements is the first step to learning TCM wellness.

Yin and Yang are two fundamental attributes of all things in the universe. They are mutually opposing, interdependent, and transform into each other. Yin-Yang balance is the foundation of health; imbalance leads to disease.
The human body is also a microcosm with Yin-Yang aspects:

items: Back is Yang, abdomen is Yin
Six Fu organs are Yang, Five Zang organs are Yin
Qi is Yang, Blood is Yin
Function is Yang, substance is Yin

en.text: The Five Elements are Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water. They interact through generation and restriction cycles, forming the basis of universal transformation.
Generation means one element nurtures and supports another:

items: Wood generates Fire: Wood provides fuel
Fire generates Earth: Fire turns to ash
Earth generates Metal: Earth contains minerals
Metal generates Water: Metal condenses into liquid
Water generates Wood: Water nourishes growth

en.text: Restriction means one element controls or limits another:

items: Wood restricts Earth: Roots break through soil
Earth restricts Water: Earth holds back water
Water restricts Fire: Water extinguishes fire
Fire restricts Metal: Fire melts metal
Metal restricts Wood: Metal cuts wood

en.text: TCM correlates the Five Zang organs with Five Elements:

items: Governns free flow: regulates Qi and emotions
Wood type: Eat green foods, stay emotionally balanced
Fire type: Eat red foods, avoid emotional agitation
Earth type: Eat yellow foods, regular meals
Metal type: Eat white foods, keep airways clear
Water type: Eat black foods, moderate sexual activity

en.text: When Five Elements are imbalanced, health issues arise requiring targeted regulation:

items: Excess Wood (Liver): Irritability, headaches, insomnia → Soothe Liver
Excess Fire (Heart): Dry mouth, anxiety, mouth ulcers → Clear Heart Fire
Deficient Earth (Spleen): Fatigue, bloating, loose stool → Strengthen Spleen
Deficient Metal (Lung): Cough, shortness of breath → Tonify Lung Qi
Deficient Water (Kidney): Back pain, tinnitus, hair loss → Nourish Kidney Yin

en.text: Note: Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory is profound. Mastery requires long-term study and practice. Wellness regulation should be personalized. Consult a qualified TCM practitioner for guidance.
